question_text: Why do modern LED light bulbs have higher efficiency than older incandescent bulbs?
correct_answer: LED bulbs convert a much higher proportion of electrical energy into light rather than heat. Incandescent bulbs waste about 90% of their energy as heat, while LEDs waste much less. LEDs use a different mechanism (light emission from semiconductors) rather than heating a filament.
explanation: Incandescent bulbs work by heating a wire until it glows, which wastes enormous amounts of energy as unwanted heat. LEDs directly emit light using a semiconductor, so they're designed to produce light efficiently. This is why LEDs are better for saving energy and reducing electricity bills.
